name: Embalse de Agrio de Aznalcóllar from El Castillo de las Guardias – circular
name_original: Embalse de Agrio de Aznalcóllar desde El Castillo de las Guardias – circular
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around El Castillo De Las Guardas are situated in the first foothills of Seville's Sierra Morena. The landscape features vast pastures where fighting bulls graze, alongside pine trees, holm oaks, and cork oaks. The region is characterized by river valleys, including the Guadiamar and Agrio rivers, and offers a natural corridor with varied terrain suitable for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around El Castillo De Las Guardas The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Agrio de Aznalcóllar from El Castillo de las Guardias – circular, a 47.2 miles (76.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 4 minutes to complete. This difficult route winds through varied landscapes including river valleys and open pastures. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía Verde de El Ronquillo - Water Route - circular, a difficult 28.7 miles (46.1 km) path. This route features a compacted dirt track, known as a "Vía Verde," and passes by water bodies. Local touring cyclists also love the La Minilla Reservoir – La Minilla Reservoir loop from Embalse de la Minilla, a 17.9 miles (28.8 km) trail leading through pastures and holm oak forests, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around El Castillo De Las Guardas is defined by the foothills of Sierra Morena, vast pastures, and river valleys. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in El Castillo De Las Guardas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Castillo De Las Guardas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in El Castillo De Las Guardas?
A: There are over 30 touring cycling routes in El Castillo De Las Guardas, offering a variety of experiences. These routes are situated in the first foothills of Seville's Sierra Morena, providing diverse landscapes for exploration.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in El Castillo De Las Guardas?
A: The terrain in El Castillo De Las Guardas is quite varied, ranging from vast pastures where fighting bulls graze to areas with pine, holm, and cork oaks. You'll also find river valleys, including the Guadiamar and Agrio rivers, the latter known for its uniquely colored waters due to historical mining. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988052742/via-verde-de-el-ronquillo-water-route-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de El Ronquillo - Water Route - circular&lt;/a&gt;, incorporate compacted dirt tracks, offering a smooth biking experience.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, El Castillo De Las Guardas offers several circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990379889/embalse-de-agrio-de-aznalcollar-from-el-castillo-de-las-guardias-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Agrio de Aznalcóllar from El Castillo de las Guardias – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 76 km trail. Another well-liked circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31858310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir – La Minilla Reservoir loop from Embalse de la Minilla&lt;/a&gt;, covering 28.8 km through pastures and holm oak forests.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while cycling in El Castillo De Las Guardas?
A: While cycling, you can encounter a variety of natural features and historical landmarks. The region is home to the Guadiamar and Agrio rivers, and you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2710424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Minilla Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3967733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites include the remnants of the castle and megaliths, and you can enjoy panoramic views from the Cerro del Castillo Viewpoint. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4402997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable natural monument nearby.

Q: Are there any family-friendly cycling options in El Castillo De Las Guardas?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, the region's Vía Verde sections, like those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988052742/via-verde-de-el-ronquillo-water-route-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de El Ronquillo - Water Route - circular&lt;/a&gt;, offer compacted dirt tracks that can be more suitable for families. Additionally, the El Castillo de las Guardas Nature Reserve provides a unique safari-like experience that families might enjoy, though cycling within the reserve itself may be restricted to specific areas or not permitted.

Q: What are some of the more challenging touring cycling routes around El Castillo De Las Guardas?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990379889/embalse-de-agrio-de-aznalcollar-from-el-castillo-de-las-guardias-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Agrio de Aznalcóllar from El Castillo de las Guardias – circular&lt;/a&gt; are ideal. This route spans nearly 76 km with significant elevation changes. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31860334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Chiclana – Agrio River – Widest Area loop from La Rosaleda&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 45.1 km and features substantial climbs.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in El Castillo De Las Guardas?
A: The touring cycling routes in El Castillo De Las Guardas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the region's 'nature in its purest form,' highlighting the picturesque rural settings, diverse landscapes of pastures and forests, and the tranquil escape offered by the river valleys.

Q: Is there public transport access to the cycling routes in El Castillo De Las Guardas?
A: The region is well-connected by a network of trails that historically linked local villages. While specific public transport links directly to trailheads are not detailed, the town of El Castillo de las Guardas itself is the primary access point, and local transport options would likely connect to or pass through the town.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes in El Castillo De Las Guardas?
A: Yes, some routes offer significant distances suitable for longer touring rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990379889/embalse-de-agrio-de-aznalcollar-from-el-castillo-de-las-guardias-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Agrio de Aznalcóllar from El Castillo de las Guardias – circular&lt;/a&gt;, at nearly 76 km, is a substantial option for a full day's ride. Another route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31512812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Tinto loop from Hotel Fort West Barranco&lt;/a&gt;, offers a challenging 49.8 km journey.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the cycling trails?
A: The region is known for its 'nature in its purest form,' with vast pastures where fighting bulls graze. The El Castillo de las Guardas Nature Reserve, located on an ancient Roman mine site, is home to over 100 species in semi-open enclosures, offering a unique opportunity to observe wildlife. While cycling, you might spot various birds and other local fauna typical of the Sierra Morena foothills.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area offers several scenic viewpoints. The Cerro del Castillo Viewpoint in the town itself provides breathtaking panoramas of the surrounding landscape. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334325" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of Zufre&lt;/a&gt; is another notable spot where you can pause and enjoy the vistas while exploring the region.

Q: Are there opportunities for other adventure sports in the area besides cycling?
A: Absolutely. El Castillo de las Guardas is a hub for multi-adventure sports. Beyond cycling, you can find opportunities for climbing, canoeing, and horse riding on the local trails. The El Castillo de las Guardas Nature Reserve also offers activities like go-karting, paintball, and even swimming with sea lions.
tips: Views of the Agrio river reservoir - its name comes from the taste of its waters, caused by the mining that has occurred in its basin for thousands of years.
A small lake where you can rest for a while in the shade enjoying views. Dirt road with easy access.
A small district through which you pass both on gravel and on the road to go to the Álamo or El Madroño. There is a fountain to stock up on water.
